claims.claim: Excited about AI × crypto convergence (agents needing wallets/keys, content provenance, verification)
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about DePIN / decentralized compute & infrastructure
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about stablecoins & onchain payments as crypto's clearest PMF
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Excited about decentralized identity / proof of personhood
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Concerned about meme-coin / pure-speculation casino dynamics
claims.label: absent

claims.claim: Concerned about centralized AI concentration (few firms owning frontier models/compute/data)
claims.label: confirmed

claims.claim: Risk-on / constructive multi-year stance driven by regulatory clarity and maturing usage
claims.label: confirmed

missed_changes: Made privacy the dominant new thesis — 'privacy will be the most important moat in crypto,' creating chain lock-in and winner-take-most dynamics (extensive posts, podcast, big-ideas piece). Not anticipated in the prediction.

Actively endorsed specific liquid tokens despite the prediction's deliberately empty token axes — announced JTO/Jito investment, praised SOL/ETH as compounding claims on future cashflows, and championed Solana, LayerZero/Zero, EigenLayer, Seismic, Aleo, NEAR.

Took an explicit stance that financial use cases (not consumer/AI-identity) will drive crypto adoption first, with consumer crypto's 'real shot' deferred until stablecoins/DeFi bring hundreds of millions onchain.
Strong public political/macro commentary (anti-wealth-tax on California, Netherlands, etc.) not part of the market-view prediction.
notes: Sector-level theses (AI×crypto, DePIN via Gensyn, stablecoins, decentralized identity via XMTP+World/KYA) and risk-on stance are all clearly confirmed; he repeatedly cites regulatory clarity and >$1T monthly stablecoin volume. Centralized-AI concern is confirmed via the 'closed labs (OpenAI/Anthropic) vs open-source funding' framing and Gensyn/Delphi. The meme-coin concern is never addressed (closest is criticism of mercenary airdrop-farming capital, but that's distinct). The biggest gap is the prediction's failure to anticipate privacy as his central new thesis and his willingness to make explicit token-level endorsements.
